visual_checks: Look for any functionality that requires multipoint gestures such as pinch-to-zoom, two-finger swipe, or multi-finger tap
Identify any path-based gestures such as swipe, drag-along-a-path, or drawing a shape to trigger an action
Check interactive maps, carousels, sliders, drawing canvases, and image galleries for gesture-dependent controls
Look for custom gesture-driven UI components such as sortable lists that require drag paths or rotational gestures
Verify whether single-pointer alternatives (e.g., tap, click, long press) are provided for every multipoint or path-based gesture
pass_conditions: All functionality operated by multipoint or path-based gestures can also be operated with a single pointer without a path-based gesture
Single-pointer alternatives such as tap buttons, increment/decrement controls, or click-based navigation are provided for carousel swipes, pinch-zoom, and drag-based reordering
Map components provide zoom-in/zoom-out buttons as alternatives to pinch-to-zoom and pan gestures
Slider controls can be operated by clicking or tapping a point on the track, or via increment/decrement buttons, not only by dragging along a path
fail_conditions: A carousel or gallery requires a swipe gesture and provides no tap or button-based alternative to advance slides
A map component requires pinch-to-zoom with no zoom buttons or other single-pointer alternative
A drawing or annotation tool requires path-based gestures and offers no alternative input method
A sortable list requires a drag-along-a-path gesture with no single-pointer alternative such as move-up/move-down buttons
A custom UI widget requires a multi-finger tap or rotation gesture with no single-pointer fallback
na_conditions: Page contains no functionality that relies on multipoint or path-based pointer gestures
ne_conditions: AI response could not be parsed
Screenshots could not be captured for visual verification
Interactive gesture-based components could not be evaluated because they require real touch or pointer input beyond what automated testing can simulate
common_mistakes: Providing keyboard alternatives but no single-pointer alternative — this criterion specifically requires a single-pointer, non-path-based alternative
Assuming swipe gestures are universally accessible — users with motor impairments may not be able to perform directional swipes
Overlooking embedded third-party map or media widgets that rely on pinch/zoom gestures without button alternatives
Confusing this criterion with keyboard accessibility — a button alternative satisfies both, but keyboard-only does not satisfy pointer gesture requirements
Forgetting that the alternative must achieve the same result, not just a partial substitute

examples.pass: A photo gallery supports swipe-to-advance but also provides visible 'Previous' and 'Next' buttons that work with a single tap or click
examples.fail: An interactive map requires pinch-to-zoom and two-finger drag to pan, with no on-screen zoom buttons or other single-pointer controls
examples.partial: A carousel provides 'Next' and 'Previous' buttons but a separate sortable list on the same page requires a drag-along-a-path gesture with no single-pointer alternative

plain_meaning: Any functionality that requires a complex gesture to operate -- multi-finger gestures like pinch-to-zoom, or path-based gestures like swipe or drawing a shape -- must also be operable with a simple single-point action such as a tap or click, unless the complex gesture is essential to the function. This ensures users who cannot perform multi-touch or path gestures can still use the feature.
scope_applies_to: Features triggered by multi-finger gestures (two-finger pinch, three-finger swipe)
Features triggered by path-based gestures (swipe, drag along a path, drawing a shape)
Custom carousels, sliders, and image viewers that rely on swipe
Custom maps or zoom features that rely on pinch
Signature pads where signing is incidental rather than essential
scope_does_not_apply_to: Single-point activations like taps, clicks, and long-press
Gestures where the path or multi-point nature is essential (drawing an actual signature, a freehand art tool)
Drag operations that can be handled as single pointer without a path (covered more specifically by SC 2.5.7)
User-agent gestures provided by the browser or OS
borderline_calibration: If the feature offers any single-pointer alternative (buttons, taps), it passes even if gestures are also available.
Essential gestures (drawing, real signatures) are exempt; do not flag them.
Only flag when the ONLY way to operate the function is a complex gesture.
A swipe carousel with visible next/previous arrow buttons passes.
